summary: No new arXiv weekend batch was visible in the literal July 4-5 window, so this run reviewed the latest posted Friday July 3 batch plus nearby high-relevance July 2 items. The strongest heavy-ion selections concern heavy quarkonium yield ratios as small- and large-system hot-medium probes, conservation-law corrections to fluctuation cumulants, realistic in-medium QCD antenna radiation, and spin-resolved femtoscopy. The strongest AI/ML-adjacent items have concrete transfer value through point-cloud heavy-flavor tagging, detector-timing PID, and calibrated uncertainty estimates for collider surrogates; generic PDE-ML claims remain cautionary without domain stress tests.
strongest_paper_summary: Subensemble Acceptance Method 3.0 and the quarkonium medium paper are the strongest heavy-ion selections: the former improves fluctuation-baseline methodology with explicit validation against canonical sampling, while the latter targets experimentally clean quarkonium ratios across p-Pb and Pb-Pb systems.

papers.title: Probing hot QCD medium with heavy quarkonium in small and large collision systems
papers.abstract: The paper evolves charmonium and bottomonium states with time-dependent Schrodinger dynamics in complex in-medium heavy-quark potentials, describing excited-to-ground quarkonium yield ratios in p-Pb and extending the framework to Pb-Pb bottomonium ratios.

papers.title: Subensemble Acceptance Method 3.0: General Corrections to Cumulants from Exact Conservation Constraints
papers.authors: Roman Poberezhnyuk, Volodymyr A. Kuznietsov, Gregoire Pihan, Volodymyr Vovchenko
papers.abstract: The paper generalizes subensemble acceptance corrections for fluctuation cumulants under multiple exact conserved charges, including energy, and validates the resulting heavy-ion fluctuation baselines against direct canonical Monte Carlo sampling.

papers.title: Gluon radiation from a QCD antenna with realistic parton-medium interactions
papers.authors: Carlota Andres, Liliana Apolinario, Nestor Armesto, Andre Cordeiro, Fabio Dominguez, Pablo Guerrero-Rodriguez, Jose Guilherme Milhano
papers.abstract: The paper formulates the in-medium gluon-emission spectrum from a quark-antiquark antenna as numerically solvable differential equations, resumming medium interactions while allowing realistic scattering models beyond the harmonic-oscillator approximation.

papers.title: Spin Femtoscopy: A Framework for Revealing Genuine Spin Correlations
papers.authors: Kehao Zhang, Xuan Wang, Xiaofeng Luo
papers.abstract: The paper proposes spin-resolved femtoscopic correlation functions, using Lambda-Lambda pairs and self-analyzing weak decays as a proof of principle, to separate genuine two-particle spin correlations from spin-dependent femtoscopic mixing.

papers.title: Heavy-Flavor Electron Classification Using Hadronic Environment as Point Cloud
papers.authors: Jingyu Zhang, Wanbing He, Long Ma
papers.abstract: The paper models the hadronic environment around heavy-flavor electrons as a point cloud and compares set-based neural architectures, including transformers, for charm-bottom electron separation, improving over a hand-crafted-observable BDT baseline.

papers.title: WavePID: Low-energy flavor identification using single-PMT time series in IceCube
papers.authors: IceCube Collaboration: R. Abbasi, M. Ackermann, J. Adams, J. A. Aguilar, M. Ahlers et al.
papers.abstract: WavePID is a template-based log-likelihood-ratio classifier using nanosecond-scale single-module timing observables, improving low-energy neutrino flavor identification when combined with graph-neural-network-selected samples.

papers.title: Local Conformal Predictions for Calibrated Surrogates
papers.authors: Suprio Dubey, Henning Bahl, Anja Butter, Jurgen Hesser, Tilman Plehn
papers.abstract: The paper introduces FALCON, a local conformal-prediction method for calibrated uncertainty intervals on neural-network surrogates for LHC scattering amplitudes and ultra-fast event generation.

papers.title: Frequency Shift Physics-Informed Extreme Learning Machine for Solving High-Frequency Partial Differential Equations
papers.authors: Xiong Xiong, Ruonan Zhai, Zheng Zeng, Sheng Zhou, Rongchun Hu, Zichen Deng
papers.abstract: The paper proposes frequency-shifted initialization for physics-informed extreme learning machines and reports large gains on high-frequency PDE benchmarks, but the transfer path to HEP or nuclear production problems remains generic.
